label.name: Name
label.tips:
How to use this node to map a new template

  1. Connect this node's output to a Debug node (set to "complete msg object").
  2. Let the stove send one data frame.
  3. In msg.payload.flat_dec you see every position as a decimal integer.
  4. In msg.payload.flat_div2 you see every position divided by 2 (temperature encoding).
  5. In msg.payload.positions[N].maps you see which built-in map label matches index N.
  6. Use msg.payload._raw for the original pipe-separated string.

Once you have identified a field, add it to your custom template JSON with the correct pos, type and (if needed) map.
label.help:
A read-only diagnostic node that subscribes to the MCZ cloud and, for every incoming raw frame, emits a detailed breakdown of all pipe-separated positions so you can identify unknown fields and build a custom template.

Output (msg.payload)

_raw
The original raw string received from the MCZ cloud (e.g. C|00|05|4A|…).
_length
Number of pipe-separated tokens in the frame.
positions
Object keyed by index (0-based). Each entry contains:
  • hex – raw hex token
  • dec – decimal integer
  • dec_div2 – dec ÷ 2 (temperature encoding used by MCZ)
  • bool – true if non-zero
  • absent_temp – true if value ≥ 0xFE (missing probe sentinel)
  • maps – for every built-in map the matching label or null
flat_dec
Flat array of { i, hex, dec } — quick numeric overview.
flat_div2
Flat array of { i, hex, div2 } — quick temperature overview.
map_names
List of all built-in map names available for lookup.

Workflow

  1. Wire the output into a standard debug node.
  2. Look at flat_dec to spot values that change when you change a setting on the stove.
  3. Note the index i — that is your template pos.
  4. Choose the right type (int, temp, bool, map, …).
  5. Add the field to your custom template JSON and load it via mcz-config.

Notes

  • This node has no input; it subscribes directly to the config node.
  • It does not parse data through any template.
  • Leave it connected only while mapping; remove it from production flows.
